items.title: A look at how each NBA champion over the past decade utilized their cap space.
items.body_excerpt:
A look at how each NBA champion over the past decade utilized their cap space. 

Top-3 cap hits are highlighted for reference. 

The total % of the league cap the top three players take up is the bold valve to the left of the bar.

images.alt:
Horizontal stacked bar chart titled "How NBA Champions Spend the Cap," showing each player's percentage of the league salary cap for 11 championship teams. Bars are sorted from most recent (top) to oldest (bottom). The bold value is the combined cap percentage of the top three players. Teams and their top three:

Knicks (2025-26): 82.5% — Towns 34.36%, Anunoby 25.59%, Brunson 22.60%
Thunder (2024-25): 58.9% — Gilgeous-Alexander 25.51%, Hartenstein 21.34%, Dort 12.09%
Celtics (2023-24): 78.1% — Holiday 27.66%, Porzingis 26.48%, Tatum 23.97%
Nuggets (2022-23): 77.8% — Jokic 27.19%, Murray 25.60%, Porter Jr. 25.00%
Warriors (2021-22): 102.6% — Curry 40.73%, Thompson 33.79%, Wiggins 28.09%
Bucks (2020-21): 80.3% — Middleton 30.28%, Antetokounmpo 25.22%, Holiday 24.76%
